# content-type

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Create and parse HTTP Content-Type header according to RFC 7231

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install content-type
```

## API

```js
var contentType = require('content-type')
```

### contentType.parse(string)

```js
var obj = contentType.parse('text/html; charset=utf-8')
```

Parse a content type string. This will return an object with the following
properties (examples are shown for the string `'text/html; charset=utf-8'`):

 - `type`: The media type (the type and subtype, always lower case).
   Example: `'text/html'`

 - `parameters`: An object of the parameters in the media type (name of parameter
   always lower case). Example: `{charset: 'utf-8'}`

### contentType.parse(req|res)

```js
var obj = contentType.parse(req)
```

Parse the `Content-Type` header from the given `req` or `res` object. This
will return an object as described in `contentType.parse(string)`.

### contentType.format(obj)

```js
var str = contentType.format({type: 'text/html', parameters: {charset: 'utf-8'}})
```

Format an object into a content type string. This will return a string of the
content type for the given object with the following properties:

 - `type`: The media type (will be lower-cased).
   Example: `'text/html'`

 - `parameters`: An object of the parameters in the media type (name of the
   parameter will be lower-cased, values will remain as-is).
   Example: `{charset: 'UTF-8'}`

## Examples

### Parse from a string

```js
var contentType = require('content-type')
var obj = contentType.parse('text/html; charset=utf-8')

// obj = {
//   type: 'text/html',
//   parameters: { charset: 'utf-8' }
// }
```

### Parse from a request object

```js
var contentType = require('content-type')
var http = require('http')

var server = http.createServer(function (req, res) {
  var obj = contentType.parse(req)
  // obj.type === 'application/json'
  // obj.parameters.charset === 'utf-8'
})

server.listen(3000)
```

### Format an object to a string

```js
var contentType = require('content-type')
var str = contentType.format({type: 'text/html', parameters: {charset: 'utf-8'}})
// str === 'text/html; charset=utf-8'
```

### Use with Express.js

```js
var contentType = require('content-type')
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

app.use(function (req, res, next) {
  try {
    var obj = contentType.parse(req)
    req.contentType = obj.type
    req.charset = obj.parameters.charset
  } catch (e) {
    // Silently handle invalid Content-Type headers
  }
  next()
})

app.post('/api/users', function (req, res) {
  // req.contentType === 'application/json'
  // req.charset === 'utf-8'
  // ...
  res.json({ success: true })
})

app.listen(3000)
```
